sentences.text: Hey.
Morning.
What's going on?
Not too much, how you doing?
Man, how are you?
Not too bad.
So these guys, they've seen IQ before, or is it….
Yeah, yeah, they, … we did, … a quick IQ and, like, revenue management demo with Jennifer.
Okay, cool.
… And then… Callie is the one that we're doing it for today.
She was, like, … I guess she connected with Jennifer, and then reached out and wanted a, a demo of it.
Yeah.
I think this one's pretty much like ours to lose.
Cool.
It'll be, like, a full demo, like, I mean, all four products, basically, right?
… I'm sorry, you broke up, what'd you ask?
Sorry, it'll be, like, rev management included here, this demo?
… maybe, we'll have to see.
So, Callie's Director of Development.
Okay.
They're gonna move on IQ first, and then I think, later on, maybe a couple months from now, they're gonna do Revenue Management.
Okay, cool.
Yeah.
Cool.
Yeah, I was talking to Megan on Friday, and it sounded like she thought they were gonna pilot IQ, or pilot rev management.
So… yeah, they might, in a couple of months.
I think they want to move on IQ first.
Yeah, that's even… I mean, that's better for us.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, I don't want to slow it down.
Okay, I will let them in.
Sweet.
Hey, good morning, Callie, how are you?
Hi, good!
How are you?
I'm doing good.
Awesome.
Thanks for, reaching out to get this call scheduled.
I know we had met with, Jennifer, and she was extremely excited.
I think she might be joining the call today, but … got great feedback from her, so definitely interested to get, your feedback and the team's feedback after today.
Yeah, for sure.
So we… just to, kind of catch you up, Jennifer told us about this software, and she, you know, mentioned that it may be helpful for us on the development side, not just property management.
So myself and Jimmy, and John and Dean work on the development side, too, so we just wanted to see what it could offer, on that side.
So, no need to go… you can go into, you know, show us a little bit of what it's doing on the property management side as well.
I know Jennifer has already had some of those discussions, so, we can focus on, the development side.
Yeah, totally.
That makes sense.
Thanks for the, bit of color there.
Yeah, we could, what, … I'm curious right now, assuming you guys are probably using, like, CoStar for the development side.
Yes.
Okay.
Looking to make a switch off of that, or just want to see what else is out there, based off what Jennifer told you?
Yeah, we just want to see what's out there, based off what Jennifer said.
She said y'all had a lot of similarities, to CoStar, so we're… we've been using CoStar for many years, probably, you know, tell us how y'all compare, what… what's different about you guys, and….
Sure.
Yeah, totally.
I would say, as far as, like, rent comp data goes, we're gonna have… not even comparable to them, way better.
Our data updates every single day.
It's all 100% publicly sourced, so no need for any concerns of, like, private data sharing or anything like that.
I think the biggest consensus that Justin and I hear on all these calls is no one really is in love with CoStar.
I think there's bits and pieces of the data that people like, but a lot of the times, all these companies we're talking to.
they're actually using, like, 3, 4, 5 different data sources, and kind of having to piece together all the gaps that each one is missing.
I think that's where ApartmentIQ kind of comes in to where we can help bridge all those gaps.
We used to just be, like, an automated market survey tool.

But, with some of our biggest partners we've landed over the last 2 years, we've actually built out the acquisitions and new development piece, we've also built out a market, sub-market level data, as well as the live data feed that you get for all of your owned or managed assets for rent comp analysis.
So we have 3 different functionalities.
I'll take you through each one, briefly.
But just so you're aware, company's been around for the last 5 years.
I think we're tracking about, like, 30-plus million units every single night.
So the data that you're looking at is live.
However, you could aggregate that back over the last 5 years.

So one functionality is great for day-to-day operations, so this would fall under Jennifer's bucket, but you get a live data feed into your owned or managed assets, and each specific comp you're tracking for each, so when you log in, you're getting a live data feed to see where your unit and floor plans are positioned within that specific comp set.

For you guys on the acquisition and development side, this is great because you can query our entire database and spin up these custom market surveys for any sort of acquisition or plot of land you're looking to develop on, and you'll get all the same Brent Comp data as you would, for that hypothetical asset.
So, we'll take you through that.
Takes about 3 seconds.
You can create, as many as you'd like, and again, these will continue to update every single day as well.
Which is great.
Last, it's gonna be similar to CoStar, it's all market, sub-market level information.
You can take that down to the neighborhood, create custom boundaries, different mile radiuses, and what you're gonna get there is, rent comp data for that market, year-over-year rent growth.
We're gonna break everything down by the bed type.
You'll get 5 years of historicals that you can leverage for seasonality and trends at the market or sub-market level.
And then we're also gonna have all new construction, pipeline, the different stages that it's in.
And then the last thing in there is gonna be your, …
… demographic information.
Sorry, need another cup of coffee.
Demographic information to help you understand your renter profiles.
And where do you get that demographic information?
Is it census data?
Is it….
Yeah.
It's gonna be, census data.
I think, like, Bureau Statistics, and then, maybe one more public, source, but everything in the platform is all… it's all public information.

So the rent comp's coming from the online leasing section of the property websites, the demographics we just went over, and then all the new supplies, like building permits, government databases, things like that.
Well, sounds… Sounds like it has a lot of the same tools.
Love to see if you could share and kind of walk us through a few things, that'd be great.
Yeah, totally.
For sure.

Yeah, so I'll take you through it, and I'm… I'm actually going to start with kind of like the, the CoStar-esque tool that we have, but just so you know, and Hunter was kind of describing it there, you know, there's 3 main functionalities within the platform.
The first one, you know, would be more of Jennifer's alley, but it's the My Properties section, and this would be, you know, the properties, or I guess the portfolios market surveys.
Cool.
If I navigate over here on the left, I think what's going to be most important for your group is going to be Research and Explore.
So, I'm going to start with Explore, and again, this is going to be that market sub-market information.
But like Hunter was saying, from here, you can pipe in any city, town, neighborhood, zip code, county, … You can draw your own boundaries if you'd like.
So I use Phoenix, Hunter and I are both based out of Phoenix, but I typed in Phoenix, Arizona, and for the Phoenix city limits, you know, we're tracking about a quarter million units.
what's nice here, and I'm thinking pretty similar to CoStar, is that we have pretty much every filtering capability possible.
So you can look at vintage, you can look at, you know, total units.

If you want to look at management company, ownership company, you want to look at property class construction status, you know, if you only want to look at properties with certain occupancy, concession percentages, etc, we let you really slice and dice this however you'd like.
But inside of here, we're gonna give you that rent comp data.
So, we'll give you, first off, historical rent growth.
Which, as you can see here, can be grouped, you know, quarterly, monthly, yearly.
You can look at both gross and net rents.
You can do it by bed group if you'd like.
We're also gonna have a 30-day snapshot, which includes performance metrics and averages broken up by unit type.
Hey, guy, sorry to jump in here on the fly.
Can you do this live for any market?
I like looking at this stuff in markets that we're in, just because it's more relatable to us.
Yeah, which one you got?
Which one do you have in mind?
Either Baton Rouge or Panhandle of Florida is fine.
Yeah, it's too Baton Rouge.
I was just talking to my buddy, I, big college football fan, and LSU's on the, top of my bucket list.
I'm a Penn State guy, so… I don't know if I'll ever make it down there.
You should.
Don't, don't come until, like, October.
I know, yeah, we're in, we're in, like I said, we're in Phoenix, so it's… it was 118 here last week, so I feel ya.
So yeah, Baton Rouge, I pulled it up.
In Baton Rouge, we're tracking 450 properties, about 60,000 units.
So, for those 60,000 units, we're looking at, again, your historical rent growth.
We have those averages broken out by bed type.
And this is… sorry to interject, and this is… this is all the entire market.
We didn't select, like, a certain class or anything.
This is everything.
Do you want to add a class, or vintage, or anything?
I was just curious.
Yeah, this is just everything in Baton Rouge, and I mean, this is even gonna include, like.
Properties with less than 50 units, so… Yeah, I mean, I just toggled on for the sake of the demo here.
I just toggled on properties with at least 100 units, which cuts us down by 10,000 units.

But, yeah, you can… I mean, if you want to look at, I know, as far as new development goes, so for your use case, if you say, hey, we only care about properties built from 2024 and newer, that are Class A, that have, you know, two bedrooms ranging from, you know, $1,500 a month to $2,000 a month, you can, yeah, you can absolutely manipulate this based off of whatever story you're trying to tell.
Right.
So, yeah, so this is the rent comp data.
Hunter mentioned this, but we do let you leverage our 5 years' worth of historical data right into the application.
So, from a historical perspective, you can look at rent trends, so again, both gross and net rents.
You can look at supply by bed type.
Leasing velocity by bed type.
And then also your occupancy, lease, and exposure percentage trends.
If you look at the map, then you'll notice 4 different colored pins on the map.
So the black pins, they represent properties that exist.
And then the blue, pink, and yellow pins actually represent properties that are under different stages of construction.
If you see here, you put your mouse over the pin, it will actually give you details on the construction project.
And then we do have a tab over here on the left dedicated to new supply.
So you can see how… sorry, question?
Yeah, sorry, you're pulling those and planning progress, you said, from government, permits.
So that is… is that everybody that has… Like, what gets you a blue diamond?
Yeah, good question.
So, we have 3 sources for, … for new supply.
So, one is going to be building permits.
Two, we also, partner with a third party called Build Central.
And then three, we do have a small human-led research team that'll look at news publications and press releases.
But as far as letting you know, kind of, the different stages, you can see here that we do offer a tooltip.
So the planning or approval stage is, … You know, you can see here, it's something that's in planning or waiting approval, has no proposed start or end construction date.
Got it.
Yep.
So, that is the rent comp data, and then lastly will be, your demographic information.
So, population, education, income, employment data.

what I've heard that's unique about our demographic data is that rather than just giving it to you at the market or sub-market level, you can actually take it all the way down to a neighborhood or custom-defined area.

So again, if you are looking to develop in an area, rather than just getting it for all of Baton Rouge, you can look at it based off of, you know, your certain pocket, or even a certain radius around your actual cross streets for the plot of land.
Can you do that based on 135, or, like, mile radius and drive time?
Is it just mile radius?
Yeah, right now it's just mile radius, but we're gonna be adding in drive time by the end of the year.
Okay, great.
I know sometimes we run into that in our, markets that are along the coast, where half of the… half of the 3 mile or 5 mile is in the ocean, so….
Yep, exactly.

For what it's worth, too, so I'm showing you this data based off of, you know, specific markets or sub-markets, you can type in a, a property name, a property address, a plot of land, or even a point of interest.
So if you want to get all this data from, you know, a 3-mile radius from LSU, you can get all that data.
Or you want to get it from a 3-mile radius from a, you know, a, a Trader Joe's, or Sprouts, or whatever it is, you can type in point of interest here and get that database off of a radius as well.
So, kind of cool.
You may get there, so I'm sorry if I'm jumping ahead.
Could you show us the screen for a specific property?
Yeah, for sure.
I'll get that, I'll get that to you next, actually.
Yeah, I just want to show you one other thing here.
Yeah, and what I wanted to show you is, if I actually click the Properties tab here, what we do is we give you the details for every property in your defined area.
So you can, rather than having to kind of put your mouse over the pen and see who they are, you just get the list view here.
I think what….
all two references.
Yeah, I think what's nice, if you click into one, so if we click into the Everly, for example.
we're gonna give you this property card.
And this is gonna be a brand new feature that we're releasing at the end of the month here.
The reason I'm showing this to you is because this is going to kind of complete that CoStar kind of replacement story that we're trying to tell.
So, like Hunter was saying, we already have better rent comp data than them.
What we're going to be adding in, though, is ownership group information, ownership history, Transactional data, building specs.
We're also going to be having property, unit, and amenity-specific images.
From here, we're going to let you compare this property versus direct competitors in the market, so you can pick the performance.
Do you show their direct onset?

We will, yeah, yeah, if you keep scrolling, what's nice here… so I'll show you this quickly, but what's nice is we're gonna lay out unit mix really well, so you get your 1s, twos, and threes, but if you want, you're gonna be able to expand and get the details on each.
Okay.
And then right below here, this is where you're going to kind of get that market survey.
Got it.

So, our system, from this view, will pull in what we consider to be the best comps, but again, you can easily kind of compare, you can, you know, pick and choose your metrics, you can see where everything stacks up.
And then if you keep scrolling, this is where you're gonna get that transactional history that I was mentioning, which will include cap rates.
So, this will be out by the end of the month, I'm just showing it to you because I think it is relevant.
Yeah, where are… I mean, we use that, honestly, every day on Coastal, so where are you getting, your calf rate?
I know you're… the… y'all are kind of built on this public data, so where are you getting that?
Yeah, for cap rates, we partner with RCA, with Real Capital Analytics.
Which I think they're, you know, probably one of the most trusted sources out there, so it should be… Perfect.
And just as far as you know, for transactional history cap rates, we'll have data on every property going back to 2002 that was, traded for over $2.
5 million.
So coverage should be… should be perfect.
Okay.
I'm sure y'all hear this a lot, but y'all are really taking on CoStar head on.
Yeah, I mean, it's interesting, like Connor was saying, I mean.
everybody we talk to, they have 4 or 5 different data sources, they try to make sense of it, so we just thought that we could kind of be, like, that one-stop shop for multifamily.

So, really excited about it, and I think, like, a cool story we always tell, or I always tell, is that, you know, we work with for the top 5 in the NMHC, for the top 5 for the NMHC for third-party managers.
And over the last year, with where this product's at right now, plus our immediate roadmap, they've felt comfortable enough not renewing their CoStar subscription.
So, I think it's, … like I said, it's going to be that true replacement.
We actually just hired one of their economists, he started about 2 months ago, so we're going to be rolling out sales comp information, we're going to be rolling out forecasting.
So these are all things that are coming within the matter of this quarter, so super excited about it.
Right.
Yeah, so for new supply, here's where you can kind of pick your… your radius.
But we'll give you all the new supply detail, and then also demographics as well.
Final thing I want to show you, which I think might be helpful, is we have this market comparison dashboard.
So, from here.
I guess what I'm going to show you here is basically my view is showing you the top 50 MSAs in the country.
You can compare these markets however you'd like.

Like, if you want to compare zip codes in Baton Rouge, or you want to compare neighborhoods in Baton Rouge, or you want to compare whatever market, you know, whatever level of detail you want at the market level.
We make it easy to compare.
And then, as you can see here, we're gonna give you essentially every detail you want on that market over whatever timeframe you want as well.
So, really easy just to kind of keep track of a market at a high level.
That's going to include supply.
I don't include the breakdown, as well as rent growth, which will be color-coded based off of performance.
The forecasting details will be in here as well.
sales volume.
So, this is, I think, a nice feature, too, if you are looking at perhaps developing in different markets.
Can you put in, basically, you know, parameters of these, and it spit out markets that meet those parameters?
You can't right now, like a query builder type thing?
Yeah, you can't right now, but I know that's something that the team's looking into doing.
Okay.
Yep, good question.
But, yeah, so this is Explore, like I said, I think it'll be helpful.
The other piece of what might be beneficial, and just so I'm aware, you guys just focus on development, or do you also do acquisitions?
We do that.
Okay, so research, this will be beneficial for both.

I'll show it to you from an acquisition perspective first, but what you can do is just click on New Report, and from here, you can type in the name or the address of any property in the country, you find it in the list, you click on it, and in 3 seconds, you're gonna have a real-time market survey for that property.

So, I'll let you know, whenever you create one of these reports, our system will default to pull in what we consider to be the best nine comps, but with this edit comp button in the top right, you can add and remove whoever is relevant to your story.

So, the way the edit comp button works is on the right, you get the orange pin, which is your target property, the white pins are properties already added to your comp set, and then the black pins are properties in the area you might want to consider adding as well.
Okay, I like this comp score.
How does that… how is that made up?
Okay, good question.
So the comp score in the list view here, it's gonna be a correlation score, but what we do is we base the score off of the fundamentals of the properties in the area compared to your target.
So we'll look at things like distance or proximity, we'll look at vintage, and then we also look at total units, asset class, do some pricing and amenity matching as well.
So it's a 6 combination algorithm, distance, vintage, and price being weighted the heaviest.
Okay, great.
Yeah, totally up to you.
I think what's nice, too, is you want to track 5 properties, you want to track 15, it's totally up to you in regards to how many you track.
And then these… this is what… I'm guessing this is what Jennifer is getting on our… or, like, does it differ?
Yeah, the reporting from, like, the operations side's gonna be the exact same, it's just that, like, those will be pre-built for you, they'll always live there.
We can give access to whoever's relevant to the data.
But, yeah, basically this research tool allows you to create those for any property in the country, rather than just being based off of your own portfolio.
Okay.
Yep.
Yeah, and again, the data will be live, but we do give you the ability to aggregate it over whatever timeframe you're interested in.
We have quite a few customizable metrics, so you can see here, I don't even have The majority of them toggled on, but you can actually pick and choose whatever data points you want added in here.

I think what's really nice, too, is that since this is publicly available data, we can trace all this information back to the source, so if you're confused with maybe where a concession or how an occupancy percentage is being calculated, we can let you know.
I think what else is really helpful is that all these metrics down below here, you can actually click and drill into them.
So, for example, here, I'm going to toggle on just a few other units here, a few other metrics here.
I was curious, how are you getting applications?
How are you getting cancels?
Yeah.
Yeah, good question.
So, all of these metrics have to do with the fact that we track unit-level data every day.
So, I think the easiest way to explain applications and cancels is if we pretend that if a unit's available on a property website today, it's going to fall under this available unit bucket.

If tomorrow that unit's gone from the property website, we're gonna assume that somebody put in an application on it, and then if that unit does not reappear on the property website within 7 days, meaning it wasn't canceled, then it becomes a lease.
So, that's the… that's how you report the transactional data using publicly available information.
Okay, I'm sure, Jennifer, y'all discussed this, so sorry, Taylor.
Yeah, that was a good question.
And I was gonna say, what's nice about this, though, is because you know, accuracy is kind of what the tools built on, but it's very easy to audit your own internal numbers.
Like, if you want to see here, like, Villa, or even here, like, what is it, the Clio, they had 15 leases over the last 30 days.
You can drill into here and see those 15 specific units that we are referencing.
So really easy to kind of audit the data, track it internally, but this is something you can drill into for every property out there, so lots of detail behind it.
So go to, go to, go to, Majin's in the Park in Baton Rouge.

And… I know that on our side, like, sometimes we don't put all the units on the website, and, you know, there are only certain amount… certain units on the website, not… there's, you know, some… Strategy behind that, so how do y'all combat that when, some management companies don't always put their available units?
Their available units on the website don't always match the correct offices.
Yeah, good question.

I think that's what makes our company pretty unique, is that with the sophistication of our scrapers, what we're using to go out there and collect that information, we can actually get around hidden availability.

So if you are hiding units for a marketing tactic, or, you know, most commonly in, like, a lease-up, we can actually get around that, and that's how you actually… that's how we give you the best performance metrics.
So, it's actually not super difficult, it's, like, just changing move-in dates, selecting different amenity packages, there's ways around it, though.
So, at Mansions, we're saying you guys have 16 available units right now.
Of those 16 that are available, 9 are vacant.
I don't even… Dean, do you have Rosen up?
Yeah, I'm trying to pull it up, I had to get any good.
And we can send you ever one of these reports afterwards to check it out, too, if you want to review it.
So, how many, …
Go, go to, … go back to Mansions.
Where's mansions?
Right here, the orange one.
Scroll down
So, are you delayed at all, or is it pretty… like, I mean, it's showing that we had 2 apps in the last 7 days, and you've got us at 0.
We update it every morning, so yeah, I'm curious when those apps might have came off, or do you know what units they were?
Somebody could probably do this faster than me.
I can… we can send you over a report if you wanna… if you wanna check it out.
Yeah, if you'll just send us a report, we'll take a look at it.
I don't know.
I'm not in it every day, so….
Yeah.
But we can look it up and see.
Kind of what the… Bizarre.
Yeah.
And then, we do have fees and deposits in here, so you can understand the ancillary fees, revenue opportunities.
We show amenity packages.
I think what's nice here, too, is if, if you look at the first column here, this is like a comp average column, so just, like, a nice us versus them comparison.
Both in-unit and community-level amenities.
We have a floor plan level detail, so again, we can lay out each unique floor plan within a competitive set.
So, for the comp set we created right here, I'm gonna change the timeframe real quick.

For the comp set that we created right here, you guys actually get the smallest one bedrooms in the market, so you're 553 feet at the Arlington A, whereas the you know, Cypress Lakes here, they have a 965 square foot one-bedroom.
So we just try to make it really easy to compare your one-bedrooms off of, like, or similar size 1 bedrooms.

… You'll get the same historical data points loaded into here, so you get your rent trends, both gross and net rents, you can compare your target property, or your subject property, versus the median of the comp set, or if you'd like, you can toggle over and get each individual's property's performance over that given time frame as well.
So, pick and choose your time frame, you can select your interval, you can look at the data at the property level, or broken up by floor plan.
But, you get rent trends, you get concession history, You'll get supply, leasing velocity.
And again, those occupancy, lease and exposure percentages.
Are y'all solely, browser-based?
Right now, we are, yeah.
Yeah, we've had some requests for the app, like, if you're out on site visiting.
Yeah.
The regional or something, yeah, so I think, I think an app will come, it's just not really in the forefront right now.
How does it render on a, like, go to a.
you know how to do mobile, like, I think you can do the little hamburger and say, request, mobile site, and it, like, puts it in a phone.
Yeah, it's not bad, you'd have to scroll a little bit, it looks okay on an iPad.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And just so you can see, too, from a new development standpoint, what you would do here is go back to Research, and you click New Report.
And then you can actually create a custom property.
So from here, if you have the, the plot of land, you can actually type in the address, or if you just know that, hey, I want to develop in Scottsdale, Arizona, you can type in Scottsdale.
You can pick your built total units, and then if you know your amenity mix, you want to have 1, 2s, and threes, the averages of each, and the rent price you're trying to achieve.
Based off of those variables, we'll generate one of those market surveys again in 3 seconds.
So, it can be really helpful to kind of see which comps out there our system thinks are strong, as well as kind of building your own, but that's kind of the use case for new development.
Do y'all have any re- or any data on land comps?
We will, not right now.
That's part of this, new release with the, with this economist we just hired.
So, that's, like, a Q4 target.
Sales comps, sales comps, forecasting.
The ownership group information that I was showing you, the cap rates, that'll be out here by the end of the month.
Can you send us, basically the new stuff you have coming on, and, like, a timeline of those things?

I know the land comp data we use very often, with our new development stuff, … But if you could just give me a timeline on when, kind of, those new rollouts, are coming, that would be really helpful for us.
For sure.
Yeah, I'm gonna get you, like, a PDF over of it.
Can you download See, I'm sure you can download all of these and save them, or download different reports, the market reports, the demographics.
We like to use these in our, you know, we download reports and use the, ….
Yeah.

Yeah, so I'll let you know, … First off, all the data I'm showing you, we can get to you in any format you want, so if you want Excel, you want PDF, if you have a BI tool or a data warehouse, we can, you know, set up API integrations, etc.
We have both these, like, pre-canned Excel and PDF reports that can be downloaded anytime you'd like.
You can also automate them to be delivered to an inbox on whatever frequency or cadence that you'd like.
Yeah, just to show you these, they're pretty slick, so if you download one of them, you get that market survey that I walked you through.
So it comes through looking, you know, pretty nice and clean, professional.
You'll get, two different floor plan reports.
So one, you'll just get the roll-up and show the averages for each bed type in a property.
You'll get the breakdown of each individual floor plan within a bed group.

What I like is inside of here, we do provide you with all of the raw unit-level data behind what's powering these reports, so if you want to do any analysis, manipulation, create pivot tables, it's just easy access, there you go.

And then we do have a visual market survey here, so this can be looked at at the property level or broken out by floor plan, but it's essentially a dozen visuals, you know, breaking out every major KPI that you could be interested in.
Bye.
So those are kind of like the pre-canned ones, but yeah, we can get you, I mean, the data however, however you want it.
But yeah, that's… that's essentially it.
That's… I promised a high level we could drag you through the weeds, but I don't know if we need to on a Monday morning.
But, yeah, big takeaway is, again, it's going to be significantly better rent comp data than you're getting with CoStar.
Ours is daily, theirs is every 4 to 6 weeks.
